specification.topic-statement: The render text utilities provide two byte-level text transformations used during terminal rendering: newline counting and newline-to-CRLF conversion.

scope.in-scope: Counting newline characters in a byte sequence, and copying a byte sequence while replacing each newline with a carriage-return–newline pair.

behavior.counting-newlines: A byte sequence and its length are provided. Each byte equal to 0x0A (newline) is counted. The total count is returned. Bytes of any other value are ignored. No allocation occurs.
behavior.crlf-conversion:
A source byte sequence, its length, and a destination buffer are provided. Each byte is copied from source to destination. When a byte equal to 0x0A (newline) is encountered, it is replaced with two bytes: 0x0D (carriage return) followed by 0x0A (newline). All other bytes are copied unchanged. The total number of bytes written to the destination is returned.
No length is accepted for the destination buffer, and no bounds checking is performed. For a source sequence containing N newlines, the destination requires up to (source_length + N) bytes. If the destination buffer is undersized, the write overruns.
